title: The Last Consider
author: Jacob Roberts
content: When a career in a high-pressure field and the cost of ambition, the consequences ripple outward in ways no one could have predicted. Set in a religious community, this darkly funny work examines the spaces between intention and impact, between the stories we tell ourselves and the truths we cannot escape. At its core, this is a story about reinvention, family, identity, and resilience. The narrative weaves these threads together with precision and emotional depth, creating a tapestry that resonates long after the final page. Each character embodies a different facet of these central concerns, their intersecting lives illuminating the complexity of human experience. Structure and voice work in concert here, the darkly funny narrative unfolding across timelines and perspectives that gradually converge. Each section adds a new dimension to the central question, building toward a resolution that feels both surprising and inevitable. In the tradition of Carmen Maria Machado and Matthew Perry, Jacob Roberts delivers a darkly funny work that refuses to look away from difficult truths. The Last Consider will appeal to readers hungry for memoir that challenges as much as it entertains, offering no easy comfort but genuine illumination. At its core, this is a story about belonging, reinvention, and growth. The narrative weaves these threads together with precision and emotional depth, creating a tapestry that resonates long after the final page. Each character embodies a different facet of these central concerns, their intersecting lives illuminating the complexity of human experience. Through the lens of truth, resilience, and trauma, the work examines how individuals navigate forces larger than themselves. The setting of the entertainment industry becomes more than backdrop; it functions as a character in its own right, shaping and constraining the choices available to those who inhabit it. Through the lens of resilience, identity, and addiction, the work examines how individuals navigate forces larger than themselves.
